data.title: Trinity Industries declares $0.30 dividend
data.description:
• Trinity Industries (NYSE:TRN) declared $0.30/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 4.3%
  • Payable Oct. 31; for shareholders of record Oct. 15; ex-div Oct. 15.
  • See TRN Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Thomson Reuters sets automatic share purchase plan
data.description:
• Thomson Reuters (NASDAQ:TRI) (TSX:TRI:CA) said it has set up an automatic share purchase plan with its broker as part of its previously announced $1 billion buyback program and normal course issuer bid (NCIB) to purchase up to 10 million common shares between August 19, 2025 and August 18, 2026.
  • The automatic share purchase plan is intended to allow for the purchase of shares under the NCIB during pre-determined times when the company would ordinarily not be permitted to purchase shares due to customary blackout periods or other regulatory restrictions. 
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Etsy appoints Rafe Colburn as chief product and technology officer
data.description:
• Etsy (NASDAQ:ETSY) on Thursday said that Rafe Colburn, the company's current Chief Technology Officer, will expand his remit to Chief Product and Technology Officer, effective September 8.
  • In this new role, Colburn will now oversee the product and engineering organizations for the Etsy marketplace.
  • He will report to Kruti Patel Goyal, Etsy's President and Chief Growth Officer, and remain part of the company's executive team.
  • This appointment follows the departure of Nick Daniel, who is leaving Etsy to pursue other opportunities.

data.title: Dragonfly Energy secures patent for Wakespeed Charge Control technology
data.description:
• Dragonfly Energy (NASDAQ:DFLI) said on Thursday that it has secured U.S. Patent No. 12,403,782 for its Wakespeed Charge Control technology.
  • The newly issued patent addresses the transfer of power from a towing vehicle to a trailer for the purpose of charging onboard batteries. 
  • Dragonfly Energy (NASDAQ:DFLI) shares were up more than 2% in premarket trading.

data.title: Mobix Labs secures AI drone contract and expands into multi-billion infrastructure market
data.description:
• Mobix Labs (NASDAQ:MOBX) Thursday announced that its Mobix Labs Wireless division, RaGE Systems, has been awarded a new contract to develop drone-based, AI-powered inspection technology for the U.S. rail industry.
  • This program marks Mobix Labs’ entry into a new market, applying its technology to improve rail safety and modernize critical infrastructure.
  • “This program shows the potential of combining drones with AI to tackle one of America’s toughest infrastructure challenges,” said Russell Cyr, General Manager and Vice President of Mobix Labs Wireless.

data.title: C2 Blockchain expands treasury with 27M more DOG coins
data.description:
• C2 Blockchain (OTCPK:CBLO) acquired 27.15M additional DOG coins, increasing its holdings to 391.51M DOG.
  • The move supports the company’s strategy to build a digital-asset-backed balance sheet.
  • DOG is a Bitcoin-native meme token built on the Runes protocol.
  • DOG transactions settle directly on Bitcoin, supporting miner fees and network security.
  • Holding DOG allows C2 to offer direct Bitcoin-layer exposure to its shareholders.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Salesforce's weak guidance shows AI is not helping enough yet: analysts
data.description:
Following weaker-than-expected guidance from Salesforce (NYSE:CRM), Wall Street analysts collectively said that the prospect and hope of artificial intelligence has not helped the company turn a corner. Yet.

Shares fell 7% in premarket trading on Thursday.

“Lack of a FY raise on a decent 2Q suggests less upside [the rest-of-year than previously expected],” Wells Fargo analyst Michael Turrin wrote in a note to clients. “Remain balanced until greater signs of a catalyst emerge, w/ Agentforce uptake proving slower than anticipated (ARR disclosure not given).” Turrin has an Equal-Weight rating and $265 price target on Salesforce.

Similar, Evercore ISI analyst Kirk Materne said investors are playing a “waiting game” with Salesforce, as they look for subscription revenue to stabilize. 

“While we did not see this quarter as the ‘unlock’ for the bull case, at these levels, we believe stabilization in 2H c/c subs revenue is ‘good enough’ to start shifting sentiment in a more positive direction,” Materne wrote in a note to investors. “That said, seeing is believing and while we believe the risk/reward continues to skew to the upside if thinking out 3 to 4 months, shares are likely in a holding pattern until Dreamforce in mid-Oct.” Materne has an Outperform rating and $360 price target on Salesforce.

Morgan Stanley analyst Keith Weiss also acknowledged that the company is “positioning for the turn,” but was a bit more optimistic in that he believes the pieces are coming together soon.

“... Within the details of the quarter, one can see the pieces coming together for a more positive trajectory in growth and a solid catalyst path ahead for the shares,” Weiss wrote.

Weiss added that while the revenue guidance was “relatively unchanged” (with foreign exchange providing a boost to the low-end), the commentary from management was more encouraging. Chief Revenue Officer Miguel Milano made several comments about the pipeline, including big deals, which are approaching 20% growth.

“Bottom line, this strong positioning to benefit from the expanded capabilities of GenAI remains unappreciated in a marketplace thinking “SaaS is dead” and within a stock trading at 16X CY26 [free cash flow],” Weiss added. “As such, we see a positive risk/reward in CRM and remain firmly [Overweight].” Weiss also has a $405 price target on Salesforce.

data.title: VinFast Auto GAAP EPS of -$0.35, revenue of $663M
data.description:
• VinFast Auto press release (NASDAQ:VFS): Q2 GAAP EPS of -$0.35.
  • Revenue of $663M (+91.6% Y/Y)
  • EV deliveries were 35,837 in the second quarter of 2025, maintaining a steady delivery rate quarter-over-quarter and representing a 172% increase year-over-year. Cumulatively, in the first half of 2025, the Company delivered 72,167 EVs to customers globally, representing a 223% increase year-over-year.
  • E-scooter and e-bike deliveries were 69,580 in the second quarter of 2025, representing a 55% increase quarter-over-quarter and a 432% increase year-over-year. Cumulatively, in the first half of 2025, the Company delivered 114,484 e-scooters and e-bikes, representing a 447% increase year-over-year.

More on VinFast Auto

data.title: Job cuts climb 39% in August, boosting YTD total higher than all of 2024
data.description:
U.S.-based employers announced 85,979 job cuts in August, up 39% from July and 13% higher than August 2024, Challenger, Gray & Christmas said on Thursday.

So far in 2025, companies have announced 892,362 job cuts, the highest year-to-date total since 2020, when the pandemic led to 1.96M announced job cuts as entire industry sectors, such as leisure and hospitality, ground nearly to a halt.

The YTD total climbed 66% from the same period last year, and has already exceeded full-year 2024’s total by 17%.

“After the impact of DOGE on the federal government, employers are citing economic and market factors as the driver of layoffs,” said Andrew Challenger, senior vice president at Challenger, Gray & Christmas. “We’ve also seen a spike in cuts due to operation or store closings and bankruptcies this year compared to last.” 

As for hiring plans announced so far this year, employers plan to add 87,626 jobs, up 10% from the first eight months of 2024. In August, hiring plans totaled 1,494 jobs, the lowest monthly total for the month since Challenger started tracking hiring plans in 2009.

Among industry sectors, the pharmaceutical industry announced the most job cuts in August at 19,112, surging from 1,359 in July and 1,561 in August 2024. Year-to-date, the industry announced 22,433 job cuts, up 142% from the same period a year ago. 

Challenger attributes the industry’s payrolls slashing to increased competition as patents expire and refocusing on higher-profit products. Many of the companies are also applying AI, causing restructuring and leading to job loss.

The financial industrial industry announced 18,092 in job cuts, bringing the year-to-date total to 44,986, up 27% Y/Y.

Technology reported plans for 12,988 job cuts. That totals 102,239 for the year-to-date, down 3% from the same period in 2024.

Retailers’ announced job cuts for the first eight months of 2025 climbed 242% Y/Y to 83,656. That sector has been hit by tariffs, inflation, and ongoing economic uncertainty. For the month, retail job cuts were 3,169, up from 622 in July and 1,412 in August 2024.

Non-profits announced 4,325 job cuts for the month, resulting in 22,151 YTD, up more than fivefold from 4,032 in the same period last year.

More on the US Labor Market

  • July JOLTS: More Evidence That The Labor Market Weakened This Summer

  • Fed's Waller sees 'multiple' rate cuts in months ahead; sees tariffs slowing growth

  • Job openings drop in July in JOLTS report, signaling softer labor market

  • Payrolls annual benchmark revision expected to point to more subdued job creation

data.title: Burberry jumps back onto the FTSE 100 Index as the turnaround takes hold
data.description:
Burberry Group plc (OTCPK:BURBY) (OTCPK:BBRYF) has been added back to the FTSE 100 Index after an absence of one year. The index inclusion will take effect at the start of trading on September 22.

The FTSE Index reshuffle is part of a scheduled quarterly review by FTSE Russell, aiming to reflect current market capitalizations within the index.

Burberry (OTCPK:BURBY) lost its coveted FTSE 100 spot in 2024 but has since undergone a significant transformation, led by CEO Joshua Schulman.

"Over the past year, we have moved from stabilising the business to driving Burberry Forward with confidence. The improvement in our first quarter comparable sales, strength in our core categories, and uptick in brand desirability gives us conviction in the path ahead," highlighted Schulman in the company's last trading update. "Our Autumn 2025 collection is being well received by a broad range of luxury customers as it arrives in stores. Although the external environment remains challenging and we are still in the early stages of our transformation, we are encouraged by the initial progress we are starting to see," he added.

Shares of Burberry (OTCPK:BURBY) are up 29% on a year-to-date basis.
